_meta.investigation_scope: delegation and orchestration skills gap analysis

audit_results.multi_wave_detail: Lines 106-144 define full dispatch flow: Wave1(parallel hivexplorer×3)→synthesize→Wave2(parallel research)→checkpoint→Wave3(implementation)→Wave4(verification). Gate between waves. Carry-forward ≤5 items.
audit_results.hierarchical_consumption_detail: Lines 148-152: 'Each wave consumes the previous wave's synthesis', 'No skipping', 'Gates between waves', 'Carry-forward ≤5 items'
audit_results.parallel_detail: Lines 154-162: Parallel ONLY when slices share no files/state, self-contained, no import conflicts. Cross-team awareness (lines 165-171) checks git status before dispatching.
audit_results.front_agent_awareness_detail: Lines 242-258: Lineage detection (hiveminder vs hivefiver). Lines 93-104: Session state detection (fresh/resume/cancel+resume). Lines 279-300: Step-by-step protocol with phase tracking.
audit_results.packet_quality_detail: Lines 168-176: Core protocol, mode selection, structured returns. Lines 178-191: Shared return contract. HOW-TO-PROCESS vs IMPLEMENT (lines 127-166). Missing: explicit prerequisite artifact paths, parent planning governance docs.
audit_results.granularity_detail: Lines 106-111: ≤5 files per slice. Lines 98-111: Task decomposition rules. CRITICAL GAP: No >500 LOC rule anywhere in skill.
critical_gaps: Missing: >500 LOC = never in one task (mandate requirement absent)
Missing: explicit token budget / 200k context window awareness
Missing: systematic context rot handling beyond 'context drift' mention

audit_results.multi_wave_detail: Lines 268-298: Investigation swarm delegation with bounded slices, parallel within wave, synthesis between. Lines 300-316: Hierarchical consumption with wave sequencing.
audit_results.hierarchical_consumption_detail: Lines 300-316: 'Each wave feeds the next', 'Never skip to implementation without investigation+research synthesis', '≤5 carry-forward'. CRITICAL: Explicit 'no skipping' rule present.
audit_results.parallel_detail: Lines 432-442: Sequential for shared mutable state/previous evidence; parallel ONLY for isolated read-only slices. Lines 452-459: Decision tree for pattern selection.
audit_results.front_agent_awareness_detail: No lineage detection, session state tracking, or workflow phase awareness. These are exclusively in use-hivemind (parent skill).
audit_results.packet_quality_detail: Lines 127-166: HOW-TO-PROCESS vs IMPLEMENT separation (strongest in ecosystem). Lines 180-191: Shared return contract with all required fields. Lines 279-286: Swarm packet shape. Lines 172-176: Core protocol. Out-of-scope boundaries explicit. CRITICAL GAP: No parent planning/governance doc inclusion requirement.
audit_results.granularity_detail: Lines 106-111: ≤5 files per slice, concern separation (read/write/verify). CRITICAL GAP: No >500 LOC rule. Lines 169: out-of_scope field present but no explicit 'when to stop' field.
critical_gaps: Missing: >500 LOC = never in one task (mandate requirement absent)
Missing: front-agent self-awareness (delegates to use-hivemind but skill itself has no awareness mechanisms)
Missing: explicit parent planning/governance document paths in packet
Missing: formal 'when to stop' / stop condition field in packet structure

audit_results.multi_wave_detail: This skill is single-pass planning. Does NOT define multi-wave dispatch. Lines 44-56: 5-step single flow (spec→validate→decompose→dependencies→track). Lines 279-288: TDD integration shows handoff TO other skills but doesn't define the wave protocol itself.
audit_results.hierarchical_consumption_detail: Planning skill consumes inputs and produces outputs consumed BY waves. Doesn't govern wave sequencing itself.
audit_results.parallel_detail: Lines 324-330: Dependency decision tree (hard/soft/dev deps). Lines 170-171: dependency_graph with parallel_waves and critical_path. Lines 128: concern separation (read/write/verify).
audit_results.front_agent_awareness_detail: This skill is about planning logic, not session/self-awareness. Those concerns belong to use-hivemind.
audit_results.packet_quality_detail: Lines 176-198: Slice template with in_scope/out_of_scope, gate with expected_pass, evidence_required. CRITICAL GAPS: No prerequisite artifact paths, no skills_to_load field, no parent planning reference, no explicit 'when to stop'.
audit_results.granularity_detail: Lines 132: ≤5 files per slice. Line 126: Authority surface first. Line 128: Concern separation. CRITICAL GAP: No >500 LOC rule.
critical_gaps: Missing: multi-wave protocol (absent entirely - only single-pass)
Missing: >500 LOC = never in one task (mandate requirement absent)
Missing: delegation packet quality fields (prerequisites, skills_to_load, parent governance docs)
Planning output is consumed BY waves but planning skill itself doesn't define wave protocol

audit_results.multi_wave_detail: Governs iteration loops (not wave dispatch). Lines 62-69: Loop checkpoint with max_iterations, stop_conditions. Lines 85-108: Synthesis gate after each iteration. NOT the same as multi-wave dispatch but related control.
audit_results.hierarchical_consumption_detail: Lines 71-84: 'Read checkpoint before deciding next iteration', 'Sequential only - never parallel iterations', '≤5 carry-forward'. Strict sequential enforcement.
audit_results.parallel_detail: Lines 259-270: Integration verification for parallel slices. Lines 284-299: Cascading failure rules (parallel collapse >50%, same failure 3+ iterations). Advanced parallel safety.
audit_results.front_agent_awareness_detail: No lineage detection, no conversation position tracking, no phase awareness. Loop state is stored in checkpoint JSON (line 69) but skill doesn't track 'where am I in the larger workflow'.
audit_results.packet_quality_detail: Lines 110-152: Evidence-based gatekeeping with explicit evidence requirements. Lines 91-96: Four checks (carry_forward_populated, coverage_status_updated, no_contradictions, output_written). Lines 137-152: Gate evidence record format. CRITICAL GAP: Not a delegation packet skill - different purpose.
audit_results.granularity_detail: Lines 158-165: Incremental gatekeeping (file/module/phase/project levels). Lines 229-234: Scope violation detection. CRITICAL GAP: No >500 LOC rule, no file-count-based decomposition rules.
critical_gaps: Missing: >500 LOC = never in one task (mandate requirement absent)
Missing: front-agent self-awareness (no 'am I main or sub', 'how far in conversation', 'what phase')
Multi-wave protocol is iteration-level (loop control) not wave-level (wave 1→2→3→checkpoint)

requirement_1_multiwave_dispatch.description: Packet 1 → Packet 2 → Checkpoint flow (3 parallel hivexplorer waves → synthesize → sequential research waves → synthesize → planning checkpoint)
requirement_1_multiwave_dispatch.critical_missing: The exact mandate flow '3 parallel hivexplorer waves → synthesize → sequential research waves → synthesize → planning checkpoint' is fully defined only in use-hivemind. use-hivemind-planning does NOT define this - it just accepts inputs and produces a plan.

requirement_2_hierarchical_consumption.description: Must consume prior wave outputs before proceeding. 'No skipping' rule.
requirement_2_hierarchical_consumption.detail: use-hivemind lines 148-152: 'Each wave consumes previous synthesis', 'No skipping', 'Gates between waves'. use-hivemind-delegation lines 300-316: Explicit 'no skipping' rule. Strongest mandate compliance area.

requirement_3_granularity_cross_entity_c.description: Beyond surface-level parallel/sequential. Accounts for: granularity, cross-entity impact, context rot, multi-source validation, 200k context window awareness.
absent: No systematic context rot handling protocol
No token budget in parallel decision logic
requirement_3_granularity_cross_entity_c.critical_missing: The mandate requires explicit 200k context window awareness and systematic context rot handling. These are only vaguely mentioned.

requirement_4_front_agent_selfawareness.description: Am I main or sub-agent? How far into conversation? What workflow phase? What's done, what remains?
requirement_4_front_agent_selfawareness.fragmented: Self-awareness is ONLY in use-hivemind. use-hivemind-delegation and hivemind-gatekeeping have NO self-awareness - they rely on parent skill. No unified 'workflow phase tracking' across skills.
requirement_4_front_agent_selfawareness.critical_missing: No skill provides a unified mechanism for 'what phase am I in, what's done, what remains'. Phase tracking is scattered across use-hivemind (protocol), delegation (packet tracking), gatekeeping (checkpoint status).

requirement_5_packet_quality.description: Prerequisite context with validated artifact paths, skills to load, success metrics AND boundaries (what NOT to do, when to stop), parent planning and governance documents
absent: No skill explicitly requires validated artifact paths as prerequisites
No skill requires parent planning/governance docs in packet
No explicit 'when to stop' / stop condition field in delegation packets
requirement_5_packet_quality.critical_missing: The mandate explicitly requires 'Success metrics AND boundaries (what NOT to do, when to stop)'. Out-of-scope exists but no formal 'stop condition' field.

requirement_6_file_granularity.description: >3 files = must break down, >500 LOC = never in one task, parallel never for same-domain writes
present: >3 files rule: use-hivemind-delegation lines 106-111 (≤5 files per slice)
Parallel safety: use-hivemind lines 154-162, use-hivemind-delegation lines 432-442
absent: >500 LOC = never in one task is COMPLETELY ABSENT from ALL skills
requirement_6_file_granularity.critical_missing: >500 LOC rule is explicitly in mandate but absent from all 4 skills. This is the most concrete missing requirement.

top_5_findings.title: >500 LOC rule completely absent
top_5_findings.detail: The mandate explicitly requires '>500 LOC = never in one task' but this rule appears nowhere in any of the 4 skills. Only file-count rules (≤5 files) exist. This is the most concrete missing requirement.
top_5_findings.recommendation: Add explicit rule: 'If a slice exceeds 500 LOC, split by authority surface → concern → cluster until each slice is ≤500 LOC. No exceptions.'

top_5_findings.title: Multi-wave protocol not defined in planning skill
top_5_findings.detail: The exact mandate flow '3 parallel hivexplorer waves → synthesize → sequential research waves → synthesize → planning checkpoint' is fully defined only in use-hivemind (lines 106-144). use-hivemind-planning does NOT define this - it produces single-pass plans that are consumed BY waves but doesn't define the wave protocol itself.
top_5_findings.recommendation: use-hivemind-planning should explicitly reference and defer to the multi-wave protocol defined in use-hivemind, not just produce planning outputs.

top_5_findings.title: Front-agent self-awareness fragmented
top_5_findings.detail: Lineage detection, session state, and protocol steps exist ONLY in use-hivemind. use-hivemind-delegation and hivemind-gatekeeping have ZERO self-awareness mechanisms despite being core orchestration skills. The mandate requires 'Am I main or sub-agent? How far into conversation? What workflow phase? What's done, what remains?' - these are only partially addressed.
top_5_findings.recommendation: Either: (1) Add self-awareness primitives to delegation/gatekeeping, or (2) Clearly document that these skills MUST be invoked within use-hivemind's protocol and cannot operate standalone.

top_5_findings.title: Context window awareness vague - no 200k token handling
top_5_findings.detail: Context rot is mentioned once in use-hivemind (line 180) but no systematic handling. No skill addresses 200k context window awareness or provides token budget guidance for parallel operations. This is critical for token-heavy investigation swarms.
top_5_findings.recommendation: Add explicit context window awareness: 'If combined packet size approaches 150k tokens, split into smaller slices. Track carry-forward compression strictly at ≤5 items.'

top_5_findings.title: Delegation packets lack formal stop conditions
top_5_findings.detail: The mandate requires 'Success metrics AND boundaries (what NOT to do, when to stop)'. Out-of-scope fields exist but no formal 'stop condition' / 'when to stop' field in packet structure. Partial returns are handled (use-hivemind-delegation lines 231-234) but the packet itself doesn't encode explicit stop triggers.
top_5_findings.recommendation: Add explicit 'stop_conditions' array to delegation packet template: ['all files scanned', 'blocked_routes > 0', 'iteration >= max_iterations'] with explicit 'when to abort' triggers.
